title: Minimize the admission of containers with the NET_RAW capability

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers with the NET_RAW capability
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers with the potentially dangerous NET_RAW capability.
input_row.rationale: Containers run with a default set of capabilities as assigned by the Container Runtime. By default this can include potentially dangerous capabilities. With Docker as the container runtime the NET_RAW capability is enabled which may be misused by malicious containers. Ideally, all containers should drop this capability. There should be at least one admission control policy defined which does not permit containers with the NET_RAW capability. If you need to run containers with this capability, this should be defined in a separate policy and you should carefully check to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to use that policy. Impact: Pods with containers which run with the NET_RAW capability will not be permitted.
input_row.audit: List the policies in use for each namespace in the cluster, ensure that at least one policy disallows the admission of containers with the NET_RAW capability.
input_row.remediation: Add policies to each namespace in the cluster which has user workloads to restrict the admission of containers with the NET_RAW capability. Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on the creation of containers with the NET_RAW capability. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/ 2. https://www.nccgroup.trust/uk/our-research/abusing-privileged-and-unprivileged- linux-containers/
input_row.impact: Pods with containers which run with the NET_RAW capability will not be permitted.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_net_raw_capability_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all namespaces in the cluster using the Kubernetes API (`kubectl get namespaces`).
- For each namespace, check for the presence of Pod Security Admission (PSA) labels (`pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce`, `pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit`, `pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn`) set to at least `baseline` or `restricted`, as these levels restrict the NET_RAW capability by default.
- Alternatively, check for the presence of PodSecurityPolicies (if still in use) or third-party admission controllers (e.g., OPA/Gatekeeper, Kyverno) that explicitly deny the NET_RAW capability in pod specs.
- For each applicable policy (PSA, PSP, OPA, Kyverno, etc.), inspect the rules to ensure that the addition of the NET_RAW capability is denied for all pods by default, except where explicitly allowed for tightly controlled service accounts.
- Use the following kubectl commands as part of the audit:
    - `kubectl get ns --show-labels` (to check PSA labels)
    - `kubectl get psp -o yaml` (if PSPs are enabled)
    - `kubectl get constrainttemplates.constraints.gatekeeper.sh` and `kubectl get constraints` (for OPA/Gatekeeper)
    - `kubectl get clusterpolicy,policy -A -o yaml` (for Kyverno)
- Pass Criteria: Every namespace with user workloads must have at least one enforced policy (PSA, PSP, OPA, Kyverno, etc.) that denies pods with the NET_RAW capability, unless explicitly allowed for specific, controlled cases.
- Fail Criteria: Any namespace lacking such a policy, or with policies that allow NET_RAW by default, is non-compliant.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.10 PDF
